title: galium verrucosum
intro: Galium verrucosum, also known as rough bedstraw, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae, native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of gallic acid and flavonoids. The health benefits of Galium verrucosum include its potential to treat skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce inflammation and promote wound healing. In herbal preparations, Galium verrucosum is often used in teas, infusions, and tinctures, and may also be combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
